Name: Azumarill
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Azumarill from your hand to evolve 1 of your Active Pokémon, you may use this power. Each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to Azumarill. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

Name: Dark Ampharos
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Dark Ampharos is in play, whenever your opponent plays an Evolution card from his or her hand to evolve 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can’t use more than 1 Darkest Impulse Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to Dark Ampharos.

Name: Dark Crobat
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dark Crobat is your Active Pokémon, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can’t be used if Dark Crobat is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Remove from Dark Crobat a number of damage counters equal to the number of your opponent’s Pokémon in play.
Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Dark Electrode
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dark Electrode has no Energy attached to it, you may search your deck for a [D] or Dark Metal Energy and attach it to Dark Electrode. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Dark Electrode is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may move all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Dark Houndoom
Effects: Choose a card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it.
You may discard a [R] Energy or [D] Energy attached to Dark Houndoom. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack on 1 of your Pokémon in play that has Dark in its name (excluding this one). Dark Link copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Dark Hypno’s type is still [P][D].) Dark Hypno performs that attack.
Does 20 damage times the number of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.

Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Rocket’s Zapdos ex has any [D] Energy attached to it, damage done to Rocket’s Zapdos ex by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card and attach it to Rocket’s Zapdos ex.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mudkip Star
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

Name: Torchic Star
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy card attached to Torchic Star.
If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

Name: Treecko Star
Effects: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy card attached to Charmeleon.

Name: Here Comes Team Rocket!
Description: Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
